entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS
entities.description: A virus that causes clump disease in peanuts, also referred to as IPCV

entities.name: PCV
entities.description: Peanut clump virus, the general group of viruses being studied

entities.name: C-DNA PROBE
entities.description: A complementary DNA sequence used to detect specific RNA isolates of a virus

entities.name: NORTHERN DOT BLOT HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: A laboratory technique used to detect specific RNA sequences

entities.name: H-IPCV
entities.description: A specific isolate of the Indian peanut clump virus used for clone preparation

entities.name: L-IPCV
entities.description: A specific isolate of the Indian peanut clump virus used for testing hybridization

entities.name: B-IPCV
entities.description: A specific isolate of the Indian peanut clump virus used for testing hybridization

entities.name: RNA
entities.description: The ribonucleic acid of virus isolates used as the target for hybridization

entities.name: C-DNA CLONES
entities.description: Clones of the PCV genome used to develop the probe

entities.name: 2 KBP CLONE
entities.description: A specific C-DNA clone of H-IPCV with a size of 2 kilobase pairs

entities.name: 3.5-4 KBP CLONE
entities.description: A specific C-DNA clone of H-IPCV with a size of 3.5 to 4 kilobase pairs

entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The agricultural crop affected by the peanut clump virus
